pi_items.verified_value: ["Pulse Ox (Simulated)", "Infusion Pump (Simulated)"] or ["Pulse Ox (Simulated)", "Simulated Infusion Pump"]
pi_items.source: OpenICE GitHub repository analysis: https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp/tree/master/interop-lab/demo-devices/src/main/java/org/mdpnp/devices/simulation. Device model names found in source code: SimPulseOximeter.java ("Legacy Pulse Ox (Simulated)"), MultiSimPulseOximeter.java ("Pulse Ox (Simulated)"), SimInfusionPump.java ("Infusion Pump (Simulated)"), DemoSimulatedInfusionPump.java ("Simulated Infusion Pump")

OpenICE GitHub repository: PCAApplicationFactory.java specifies AppType("Infusion Safety", "NOPCA", ...). Also confirmed in web search results referencing the PCA Infusion Safety application in OpenICE documentation

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is a real, open-source medical device interoperability platform (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp) that includes simulated medical devices and clinical applications. The task uses the real OpenICE environment to create synthetic (simulated) devices and conduct a safety test. The Infusion Safety application exists and is documented in OpenICE. However, the exact device names in the metadata ("Pulse Oximeter" and "Infusion Pump") do not precisely match the actual OpenICE simulated device model names, which are "Pulse Ox (Simulated)" (and variants like "Legacy Pulse Ox (Simulated)"), and "Infusion Pump (Simulated)" or "Simulated Infusion Pump". The file paths and application name are correct. All data generated will be synthetic (from simulated devices), not real patient or device data.
